title: galium aparine f. intermedium
intro: Galium aparine f. intermedium, also known as sticky wildegarlic or cleavers, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Rubiaceae and is a variant of the Galium aparine species. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Galium aparine f. intermedium include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including skin issues, digestive problems, and respiratory infections.
